title: Scoring rework + aggregator integration + desktop orchestration + UNAVAILABLE state

files_forbidden: any component, route handler, or message catalog

acceptance_criteria: calculatePerformanceGrade no longer subtracts ANY ad-hoc LCP/CLS penalties; the headline score is round(lighthouseResult.categories.performance.score * 100). The single *100 conversion lives ONLY here.

The redundant *100 at performance-suggestions.ts (the line doing metrics.performanceScore ? Math.round(x*100)) is removed/corrected so the 0-100 integer contract holds end-to-end; performanceScore is a 0-100 integer and a genuine 0.0 lab score persists as integer 0 (NEVER null).

scoreSource is set to 'lab' on success and 'unavailable' on PSI failure. On PSI failure the result is grade 'N/A', score null, and the returned performanceMetrics object is NON-EMPTY and explicitly carries scoreSource:'unavailable' (so the downstream JSON-blob persist path cannot drop it). NEVER return grade 'F'/score 0 for a provider failure.

The mobile result is the single headline: it drives the grade, the score, and the primary CrUX verdict (Google's verbatim overall_category from T-01). The P2-07/P2-08/P2-01 modules from T-02 are imported and wired into runPerformanceModules; moduleFindingCounts is updated for the new modules. The P2-07 HIGH/banner decision is enforced mobile + URL-level only here.

Desktop orchestration: when features.desktopPerformance (T-03) is FALSE the code path is byte-identical to today's mobile-only behaviour (no second call, no desktop key in the result). When TRUE: run mobile first; if mobile is UNAVAILABLE due to 429/403 rate-limit/quota, SKIP the desktop call; otherwise run desktop. Desktop result is stored as a subordinate `desktop` sub-object in the returned performanceMetrics and NEVER averaged into the headline and never drives grade/banner.

Each form factor is independently fail-soft: a failure (including a thrown cache wrapper) for one form factor maps to that form factor's empty/UNAVAILABLE result and can never collapse the other (use allSettled-style isolation, do not rely on an implicit no-throw contract).

The PSI fetch goes through the T-04 cache wrapper, keyed per strategy, success-only, with the force-refresh/bypass option threaded for explicit re-scans; the 45000/0 timeout from T-01 is respected; when desktop is enabled a test asserts total worst-case PSI wall time stays within the documented bound under SCAN_TIMEOUT_MS.

Unit tests: double-penalty removed (0.82 score stays 82, a previously-penalized case rises), UNAVAILABLE -> N/A/null/non-empty-metrics-with-scoreSource, desktop-OFF byte-identical, desktop-ON mobile-headline + desktop subordinate, mobile-429 -> desktop skipped, one-form-factor-failure isolation. npm run lint and npm run test pass.
output_format: code plus a plain-English explanation of the new scoring formula, the UNAVAILABLE contract, and the desktop/quota/fail-soft orchestration
